question_text: Let $P = { \left( \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right) }$ and $Q = P ^ { 3 } - 2 P ^ { 2 } - 4 P + 1 3 I _ { 2 }$ , where $I _ { 2 }$ denotes the identity matrix of order 2. Then the determinant of Q is equal to (
solution:
Step 1: Characteristic Polynomial of $P$

The characteristic polynomial is found by:

 $ \operatorname* { d e t } ( P - \lambda I ) = { \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 - \lambda } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 4 - \lambda } \end{array} \right| } = ( 1 - \lambda ) ( 4 - \lambda ) + 2 = \lambda ^ { 2 } - 5 \lambda + 6 $ 

Thus, the characteristic equation is:

 $ P ^ { 2 } - 5 P + 6 I = 0 $ 

Step 2: Express $P ^ { 3 }$ and $P ^ { 2 }$ in Terms of P and I

Using the characteristic equation:

 $ P ^ { 2 } = 5 P - 6 I $ 

Multiply by $P$ to get $P ^ { 3 }$ :

 $ P ^ { 3 } = 5 P ^ { 2 } - 6 P = 5 ( 5 P - 6 I ) - 6 P = 1 9 P - 3 0 I $ 

Step 3: Simplify Q

Substitute $P ^ { 3 }$ and $P ^ { 2 }$ into $Q$ :

 $ Q = P ^ { 3 } - 2 P ^ { 2 } - 4 P + 1 3 I = ( 1 9 P - 3 0 I ) - 2 ( 5 P - 6 I ) - 4 P + 1 3 I $ 

Simplify:

 $ Q = 5 P - 5 I = 5 ( P - I ) $ 
Step 4: Compute Determinant of $Q$

First, find $P - I { \mathrm { : } }$

 $ P - I = { \left( \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right) } $ 

Then:

 $ \operatorname* { d e t } ( P - I ) = 0 \cdot 3 - 2 \cdot ( - 1 ) = 2 $ 

Finally:

 $ \operatorname* { d e t } ( Q ) = \operatorname* { d e t } ( 5 ( P - I ) ) = 5 ^ { 2 } \cdot \operatorname* { d e t } ( P - I ) = 2 5 \times 2 = 5 0 $ 

Verification

Compute Q directly:

 $ Q = { \left( \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 1 0 } \\ { - 5 } & { 1 5 } \end{array} \right) } \Longrightarrow \operatorname* { d e t } ( Q ) = 0 \cdot 1 5 - 1 0 \cdot ( - 5 ) = 5 0 $ 

Final Answer

The determinant of $Q \mathrm { i s } \Big [ 5 0 \Big ]$
Quick Tip

When dealing with matrix operations, carefully apply the basic operations and simplify the resulting matrices step by step.
